AUTOMATION.description: PC Automation: control the mouse, keyboard, and application windows. Use with desktop_screenshot for a full see-and-act loop.

commands.click: Clicks the mouse at the given screen coordinates.
commands.click_text: Locates text on the screen via OCR and clicks it.
commands.drag: Clicks and drags from one position to another (e.g. moving a file, selecting text).
commands.focus_browser_tab: Clicks on a browser tab whose title contains the given text fragment (case-insensitive).
commands.focus_window: Brings a window to the foreground by partial title match.
commands.get_browser_tabs: Returns the titles of all currently open tabs in Chrome, Edge, Opera, or Brave.
commands.get_mouse_position: Returns the current mouse cursor coordinates (x, y).
commands.get_screen_size: Returns the screen resolution (width x height in pixels).
commands.hotkey: Executes a keyboard shortcut by holding multiple keys simultaneously.
commands.list_windows: Returns a list of all currently visible window titles.
commands.maximize_window: Maximizes a window by partial title match.
commands.minimize_window: Minimizes a window by partial title match.
commands.move_to: Moves the mouse cursor to the specified screen coordinates.
commands.press_key: Presses and releases a single key.
commands.read_window_controls: Reads the UI control tree (buttons, inputs, links) of an application window.
commands.scroll: Scrolls the mouse wheel at the specified position.
commands.type_and_enter: Types the given text and immediately presses Enter.
commands.type_text: Types the given text as simulated keystrokes.

AUTOMATION.routing_instructions: VISION/AUTOMATION SYNERGY: You DO have 'eyes'! You can interact with the user's screen. When asked to click on something or read the screen, FIRST call WEBCAM__desktop_screenshot. The system will return the image to your context. THEN analyze the image to find the exact (x, y) coordinates. FINALLY, call AUTOMATION__move_to or AUTOMATION__click with those coordinates.

DASHBOARD.description: Hardware monitoring and AI backend status.

commands.get_backend_status: Verifies if the AI backend (Ollama, Kobold, or Cloud) is online and ready.
commands.get_full_dashboard: Returns a complete report of all hardware resources and AI backend status.
commands.get_gpu_status: Returns the video card (GPU) status, including VRAM usage and thermal/computational load.
commands.get_system_resources: Returns a summary of system CPU and RAM usage.

EXECUTOR.description: Safe Python execution (AST Sandbox), system control, and file management.

HELP.description: Displays the list of all active commands and modules in the system.

commands.list_capabilities: Displays the complete list of all tools, modules, and capabilities currently loaded in the system.
commands.refresh_registry: Forces a re-read and update of the central capabilities registry (rescans plugins).

MEMORY.description: Access to the Vault of memories and Admin profile (Identity and History).

commands.read_history: Extract the last N saved messages from the database history.
commands.remember_info: Save important information about the user in the biographical profile.
commands.reset_memory: Execute the Tabula Rasa protocol: clear the entire episodic chat history.
commands.who_am_i: Ask Hecos to retrieve identity data for the Admin and the AI (context profile).

MODELS.description: Changes the AI model based on the active backend (Ollama/Kobold).

commands.get_current_backend: Returns the AI backend currently in use (e.g., cloud, ollama, kobold).
commands.list_models: Lists all available models for the currently selected backend.
commands.set_model: Sets a new active AI model based on the numeric index obtained from list_models.

SYS_NET.description: Strumenti di rete avanzati: IP, Ping, Stato Proxy, Mappatura LAN e VPN/DNS.

commands.check_internet_speed: Tests the current internet download and upload speed.
commands.get_network_info: Runs ipconfig to extract local IPv4, Subnet, Default Gateway, and active DNS servers.
commands.get_proxy_status: Reports the current proxy configuration status in Hecos, including the URL and whether it is active.
commands.get_public_ip: Interrogates an external service to find the current public outbound IP address.
commands.ping_test: Executes a network ping test to the specified target (IP or domain).
commands.reset_dns: Restores the DNS configuration of the active network adapter back to DHCP (automatic).
commands.scan_local_network: Executes an ARP scan ('arp -a') to discover active devices connected to the same LAN.
commands.set_dns_servers: Changes the DNS servers of the active network adapter to bypass ISP level blocking.
commands.test_proxy: Tests the configured Proxy by attempting to reach an external service and reports the visible IP.
commands.traceroute: Performs a traceroute to show the network path and latency to a host.

WEB_UI.description: Native Web Interface — Chat + Config Panel on localhost:7070

commands.get_panel_url: Returns the URL of the chat interface.
commands.open_browser: Opens the Hecos web interface in the default browser if not already open.

commands.add_event: Creates a new calendar event. Returns a confirmation string.
commands.delete_event: Deletes a calendar event by ID.
commands.get_date_info: Returns today's date formatted natively, current holidays, and upcoming public holidays.
commands.list_events: Returns a formatted list of upcoming calendar events.
commands.sync_calendars: Manually triggers synchronization with external Google/Apple calendars.

DRIVE.description: Interfaccia Web per la gestione dei file (Upload, Download, Folder Explorer). È lo spazio di archiviazione personale dell'utente accessibile da WebUI.

commands./drive: Apre il File Manager nel browser

IMAGE_GEN.description: Generates images from text descriptions using AI image models.

commands.generate_image: Generates an image from a text description.

MEDIA_PLAYER.description: Media player: play audio/video, manage playlists via AI or dashboard.

commands.add_to_playlist: Add a file to an existing playlist (creates it if missing).
commands.create_playlist: Create a named playlist with a list of file paths.
commands.get_playlist: Show the contents of a saved playlist.
commands.get_status: Return the current playback status (track name, position, volume).
commands.list_playlists: List all saved playlists with their track count.
commands.next_track: Skip to the next track in the current queue.
commands.pause: Pause or resume the current track.
commands.play: Play a media file or URL.
commands.play_by_index: Jump directly to a track by position in the current queue (0-based).
commands.play_playlist: Load and play a saved playlist.
commands.prev_track: Go back to the previous track in the current queue.
commands.remove_from_playlist: Remove a track from a playlist by its position (1-based).
commands.scan_folder: Scan a folder for audio/video files and optionally save as a playlist.

REMINDER.description: Scheduler for time-based reminders with TTS alerts and WebUI notifications.

commands.cancel_reminder: Cancels an active reminder by its ID (full or first-8-chars prefix).
commands.list_reminders: Lists all active reminders with their scheduled time and ID.
commands.set_reminder: Creates a new reminder. Fires a TTS alert and WebUI notification when due.
commands.snooze_reminder: Postpones a reminder by the specified number of minutes from now.
commands.stop_audio: Signals the notifier to stop any currently playing or looping audio.

WEB.description: Network access for Google searches and quick opening of websites.

commands.fetch_page_content: Fetches a web page and returns its readable text content.
commands.get_clipboard: Returns the current text from the system clipboard.
commands.open_url: Opens a specific website in the default browser.
commands.search_and_read: Searches the web using DuckDuckGo and reads the text content of the top results.
commands.search_web: Opens a browser search for a query.
commands.set_clipboard: Copies the given text to the system clipboard.

WEB.routing_instructions: If the user asks you to interact with a website or application that is ALREADY OPEN on their screen (e.g., 'click the next video', 'change song', 'scroll down'), DO NOT use the WEB tools. Instead, use WEBCAM__desktop_screenshot and the AUTOMATION tools to physically interact with the existing window.

WEBCAM.description: Access to PC optical vision to take snapshots of the environment or Admin.

commands.desktop_screenshot: Takes a screenshot of the entire desktop (or a specific monitor) and saves it to disk.
commands.take_snapshot: Takes a photo using the computer's webcam and saves it to disk.

WEBCAM.routing_instructions: MULTIMODAL CAPABILITY: You DO have 'eyes'! You are natively connected to a visual multimodal core. Whenever the user asks you to look at the screen, see what is there, or analyze GUI/windows, you MUST actually execute the `desktop_screenshot` tool. The system will take the picture and feed it directly into your visual core! NEVER say you cannot see the image. Just call the tool, and in the next thought loop you will see it perfectly.
